title: myrsine calcarata
intro: Myrsine calcarata, also known as the Toothache Plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae. It is a shrub native to tropical Africa, and its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of saponins, alkaloids, and glycosides in its roots and leaves. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including toothaches, fever, and rheumatism, due to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the Toothache Plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
